rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible port names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with proper labeling and expression structure.
Generate valid AGREE contracts inside annex agree blocks with correct scope placement.
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current component scope.
Declare helper equations and constants with correct AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in dependency guarantees without referencing implementation-only paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Equations are declared as `eq name : type = expression;`.
Constants are declared as `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-specific paths in component type contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
Do not invent new identifiers; only use visible AADL features or declared helpers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Model unit and package resolution issues
